quick_facts.label: Role focus
quick_facts.value: A Secretarial Executive supports corporate-law and governance administration: maintaining statutory records, preparing meeting papers and minutes, and helping with filings and compliance tracking.

quick_facts.label: Regulatory context
quick_facts.value: The Companies Act, 2013 assigns the company secretary responsibility for reporting legal compliance to the Board and ensuring compliance with applicable secretarial standards; an entry-level executive typically assists the qualified company secretary or secretarial team with this work.

quick_facts.label: Common work outputs
quick_facts.value: Typical secretarial-team outputs include agendas and notices for Board and general meetings, minutes and statutory registers, ROC forms/returns, and supporting records for corporate approvals.

quick_facts.label: Professional pathway
quick_facts.value: The ICSI Secretarial Executive Certificate is an official recognition route for students who have passed the CS Executive Programme and completed the prescribed development programme and practical training; ICSI describes its aim as creating semi-qualified professionals for corporates and practising company secretaries.

quick_facts.label: Listed-company distinction
quick_facts.value: In a listed entity, the compliance-officer position is a regulated, qualified company-secretary role under SEBI’s LODR framework; a Secretarial Executive may support that function but should not be assumed to hold it.

sections.heading: What a Secretarial Executive does
paragraphs: This is a corporate secretarial support role rather than a substitute for the statutory company secretary. The work is centred on keeping the company’s governance calendar and records in order: preparing draft agendas, notices and explanatory material; coordinating Board, committee and shareholder meetings; capturing and maintaining minutes; and maintaining statutory registers and other secretarial records.

The role also commonly supports preparation and filing of forms, documents and returns with the Registrar of Companies (ROC), monitors due dates and approvals, and collates documents needed for compliance reviews. ICSI placement material for corporate-secretarial roles specifically identifies statutory-law compliance, filings with authorities, statutory books, secretarial records, Board meetings, ROC filings, FEMA matters and agreement drafting as relevant work areas.

For listed entities, work may additionally support disclosure and governance processes. The regulated compliance officer, however, has responsibilities under SEBI’s Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements Regulations and is distinct from a junior support position.
bullets: Maintain a compliance calendar and trackers for corporate actions, filings, registers and meeting deadlines.
Draft, format, circulate and archive meeting notices, agendas, attendance records, resolutions and minutes under supervision.
Prepare supporting data and attachments for ROC e-forms, annual-return-related work and other regulatory submissions.
Organise statutory records, Board papers and approval evidence so they are retrievable for management, auditors and regulators.
Coordinate with finance, legal, HR, directors, registrars, external practising company secretaries and government portals as required.

sections.heading: Entry routes in India
paragraphs: A practical entry route is to join a corporate secretarial department or a practising company secretary’s office in a junior documentation, compliance-support or trainee capacity while pursuing the ICSI Company Secretary qualification. ICSI’s training regulations expressly provide for practical training in companies, under company secretaries in whole-time practice, and in other eligible organisations.

For candidates following the CS route, ICSI states that Senior Secondary (10+2) students who pass CSEET may enter the CS Executive Programme; CS Foundation pass candidates and CA/CMA Final pass candidates are also listed as eligible routes. Requirements, exemptions and examination processes can change, so candidates should verify the current ICSI notification before registering.

For a recognised semi-qualified milestone, ICSI’s Secretarial Executive Certificate requires passage of the CS Executive Programme plus the prescribed Executive Development Programme/equivalent and practical training or an approved exemption.
bullets: Complete 10+2, then pursue CSEET and the ICSI CS Executive Programme, subject to the applicable ICSI rules.
Seek trainee or junior secretarial work in an in-house company-secretarial team or a practising company secretary’s firm to develop filing, records and meeting-document experience.
After meeting ICSI’s stated programme and training conditions, assess eligibility for the Secretarial Executive Certificate.

Treat employer-specific education, software and experience requirements as variable; review the actual vacancy rather than assuming that the certificate or CS student status alone guarantees eligibility.

sections.heading: Skills that matter
paragraphs: The role requires careful legal-administrative drafting and strong document control. The prescribed duties of a company secretary include facilitating and attending Board, committee and general meetings, maintaining their minutes, obtaining required approvals, and assisting the Board with corporate governance. A Secretarial Executive therefore needs to produce accurate drafts, maintain version control and handle confidential records reliably.

Working knowledge should grow around the Companies Act, applicable rules, secretarial standards, the MCA/ROC filing workflow, corporate approvals and—where relevant—SEBI listing compliance. Communication and follow-through matter because the role routinely gathers information and approvals from directors, finance, legal and external advisers.
bullets: Accuracy in names, dates, resolutions, statutory references, forms and supporting attachments.
Clear business writing for notices, agendas, minutes, resolutions, filing explanations and internal reminders.
Understanding of meeting processes, statutory registers, corporate approvals and records retention.
Ability to use spreadsheets, shared document repositories and official filing portals while maintaining audit trails.
Discretion, deadline management and the confidence to escalate missing approvals or inconsistencies to a supervisor.

sections.heading: Where the role is performed
paragraphs: Secretarial Executive work can be performed within an in-house company-secretarial or legal/compliance team, or in the office of a practising company secretary serving multiple client companies. ICSI’s practical-training framework recognises training in companies with a whole-time company secretary, under a company secretary in whole-time practice, and in other eligible bodies or organisations.

The work is predominantly office and digital-records based, with periods of heightened activity around Board and shareholder meetings, annual returns, corporate transactions and statutory filing dates. In listed entities, the team may also support stock-exchange and SEBI-related disclosure processes.
bullets: In-house secretarial, legal, governance or compliance departments.
Practising company secretary firms and corporate-law/compliance service providers.
Companies preparing ROC filings, maintaining statutory records or undertaking corporate actions.
Listed-company compliance teams, under the direction of the qualified company secretary/compliance officer.

sections.heading: How progression usually works
paragraphs: Progression is commonly built through increasing responsibility for meeting administration, statutory registers, ROC work, governance documentation and independent handling of routine compliance matters. For candidates pursuing company secretaryship, passing CS Executive and completing the prescribed development programme and practical training can lead to ICSI’s Secretarial Executive Certificate, which ICSI positions as recognition for semi-qualified professionals serving corporates and practising company secretaries.

Further progression toward the Company Secretary profession requires completion of the wider ICSI route, including the Professional Programme and the applicable training and membership requirements. The exact training structure and eligibility conditions are regulated by ICSI and should be checked against current notifications before making career or enrolment decisions.
bullets: Junior secretarial or compliance support work → broader ownership of filing, records and meeting documentation.
CS Executive pass plus prescribed development/training conditions → potential eligibility for the ICSI Secretarial Executive Certificate.
CS Professional Programme, practical experience and applicable ICSI membership conditions → pathway toward qualified company-secretary roles.
With experience, roles may expand into corporate governance, compliance, legal operations, investor/stock-exchange coordination or practice support.
